channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: China Keeps Lending Rates Flat as Producer Prices Rise
item.description: China's central bank held benchmark Loan Prime Rates steady for the eleventh month in April. The move balances strong Q1 economic growth with emerging factory-gate price increases, signaling a cautious approach amidst global economic complexities.

item.title: Analyst Sees Chart Strength in Acutaas, Prudent, Steelcast: Key Risks Noted
item.description: Analyst Aakash Shah highlights strong chart patterns for Acutaas Chemicals, Prudent Corporate Advisory Services, and Steelcast, suggesting buy opportunities with price targets. Acutaas aims for ₹2,600, Prudent for ₹3,000, and Steelcast for ₹335. Investors should also consider significant valuation premiums and mixed sector outlooks.

item.title: Gold, Silver Pullback as Dollar Gains, Yields Rise
item.description: Gold and silver prices fell as Middle East geopolitical tensions were countered by a stronger U.S. dollar and rising bond yields. Gold dipped 1.43% to $4,764.52 and silver 1.46% to $79.58, reversing recent gains. Traders are watching upcoming economic data and central bank signals for direction.

item.title: Vercel Breach: Compromised AI Tool Exposes Crypto Supply Chain Risks
item.description: Vercel, a critical infrastructure provider for web and crypto applications, confirmed a security breach originating from a compromised third-party AI tool, Context.ai. The intrusion leveraged a Google Workspace OAuth flaw to access internal Vercel systems, potentially exposing non-sensitive environment variables. This event is forcing numerous Web3 teams, including those using Vercel's popular Next.js framework, to urgently rotate API keys and audit code, highlighting increased supply chain vulnerabilities amplified by AI integration.

item.title: Indian Banks Report Solid Q4; Pharma Faces FDA Scrutiny; Infrastructure Sector Gains Projects
item.description: Leading Indian banks, including HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, and Yes Bank, reported strong Q4 earnings thanks to better net interest income and stable asset quality. Meanwhile, pharmaceutical companies Lupin and Cipla are facing scrutiny from the US FDA after facility inspections, which could affect future approvals. The infrastructure sector, however, shows positive momentum, with H G Infra Engineering and EMS winning significant new projects.

item.title: Indian Stocks Rally on Breakouts, But Valuations Raise Concerns
item.description: Indian stocks climbed on April 17, 2026, as analysts pointed to nine companies showing strong technical breakouts. However, high company valuations, sector challenges, and global risks cast doubt on whether these gains can last, prompting caution.

item.title: Gold Demand: Akshaya Tritiya Volume Dips 30% As Prices Surge 60%
item.description: Gold demand volume dropped 30% this Akshaya Tritiya as prices jumped nearly 60%. Despite fewer sales, total transaction value rose 20-25%. Consumers bought lighter jewelry, used more exchange deals (40-50% of sales), and focused on smaller coins. Investment gold and studded items also saw demand. Geopolitical concerns didn't boost gold's safe-haven appeal, with macro factors taking precedence. Analysts expect demand value to stay strong, even if sales volume remains low.

item.title: Oil Prices Jump Over $90, Lifting Asia, Worrying India
item.description: Renewed Middle East conflict sent WTI crude past $90 a barrel, driving Asian markets higher but prompting a cautious open for Indian equities. Gold prices saw a minor uptick, while silver fluctuated. Foreign investors bought Indian stocks on Friday, but domestic investors sold heavily, showing mixed sentiment amid rising geopolitical risks and potential inflation for India.

item.title: KFin Technologies Stock Surges Past ₹950 Amid Valuation Doubts
item.description: KFin Technologies' stock jumped above the ₹950 resistance mark on Friday, ending weeks of consolidation and hinting at a bullish reversal. While the technical move is a positive sign, the company's high P/E ratio compared to the IT sector and recent margin pressures mean investors face questions about whether this rally can last, even with analysts expecting significant upside.

item.title: India's $21 Billion Recycling Boom: 3 Stocks Set to Grow
item.description: India's recycling sector is poised for major growth, projected to reach $21.4 billion by 2030. Stricter e-waste and battery regulations, alongside mandates for recycled content, are driving this "urban mining" boom. Three companies—Jain Resources, Gravita India, and Pondy Oxides & Chemicals—are expanding operations and diversifying into higher-margin products to capture this growth.

item.title: Indian Stocks Surge on FII Short Cover, Midcaps Lead; Caution Remains
item.description: Indian stocks are rising as foreign investors close out bearish bets, boosting mid and small-cap shares. However, high valuations and concerns over rising costs, global events, and domestic issues cast doubt on how long the rally can last.

item.title: India's 2047 Goal Challenged by Female Workforce Gap
item.description: India's aspiration to become a developed economy by 2047, requiring sustained high growth, is increasingly challenged by a substantial gap in female labor force participation. Despite recent upticks, India's female LFPR lags global averages, representing a major untapped economic potential. Furthermore, underrepresentation in leadership roles across academia and corporate boards points to systemic issues that need addressing to unlock inclusive growth.

item.title: India's GDP Growth Figures Distorted by Outdated Price Index
item.description: India's new economic data series faces a problem: it still uses an outdated Wholesale Price Index (WPI) from 2011-12. This index struggles to reflect current prices, potentially misstating real economic growth and complicating monetary policy decisions. Experts suggest updating the WPI or using a more current Producer Price Index (PPI) for accurate figures.

item.title: Indian Firms Boost CSR Spending 23% to ₹22,212 Cr on Higher Profits
item.description: Corporate India spent ₹22,212 crore on social initiatives in FY25, a 23% jump driven by a 22% rise in company profits. While more firms participated and many boosted spending, a gap persists between required and actual outlays due to funds carried forward. Education and healthcare still lead allocations, but areas like slum development received less. This shows growing social commitment but also challenges in deploying funds effectively and broadly.
